{"id":"project-LICHEN-6kk0","title":"announce: start() not exception-safe - leaves _running=True if task creation fails","description":"File: python/src/lichen/announce/scheduler.py:189-205

The start() method sets _running=True before creating the task:

```python
async def start(self) -> None:
    if self._running:
        raise RuntimeError('scheduler already running')

    self._running = True  # Set BEFORE task creation
    self._task = asyncio.create_task(
        self._loop(),
        name=f'announce-{self.identity.iid.hex()[:8]}',
    )
```

If asyncio.create_task() raises an exception (unlikely but possible, e.g., if event loop is closed), _running remains True but _task is None.

Subsequent calls to start() would raise 'scheduler already running' even though no task exists.

Fix: Wrap in try/except and reset _running=False on failure, or set _running=True after task creation:

```python
self._task = asyncio.create_task(...)
self._running = True  # Only set after success
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:11.005454Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:11.005493Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-6kk0","title":"announce: start() not exception-safe - leaves _running=True if task creation fails","description":"File: python/src/lichen/announce/scheduler.py:189-205

The start() method sets _running=True before creating the task:

```python
async def start(self) -> None:
    if self._running:
        raise RuntimeError('scheduler already running')

    self._running = True  # Set BEFORE task creation
    self._task = asyncio.create_task(
        self._loop(),
        name=f'announce-{self.identity.iid.hex()[:8]}',
    )
```

If asyncio.create_task() raises an exception (unlikely but possible, e.g., if event loop is closed), _running remains True but _task is None.

Subsequent calls to start() would raise 'scheduler already running' even though no task exists.

Fix: Wrap in try/except and reset _running=False on failure, or set _running=True after task creation:

```python
self._task = asyncio.create_task(...)
self._running = True  # Only set after success
```","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:11.005454Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T23:40:13.436864425Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T23:40:13.436864425Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T23:45:13.436864425Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T23:40:13.436864425Z"}

new_value: Fixed start() to set _running after task creation. Commit 146b3ca
